title: Document Perception Context
description: Format of the textual description of the FreeCAD document passed to the model (the agent's 'eyes'). Stated goal: be CONCISE so as not to saturate the context of small local models. Two levels: overview (always, cheap) and detail (on-demand, chosen by the agent = geometric RAG). Open issue: empirically tune the token budget for small local models (see Phase 3 of the plan).

documentOverview.description: Overview: 'what is in the document?'. Must stay small.

bounding_box.description: overall bounding box of the document

objects.description: one synthetic line per object — NO detailed geometry here

objectDetail.description: Close-up look at a single object: emitted only when the agent requests it.

topology.description: synthetic counts, not the full mesh

named_subelements.description: relevant faces/edges the agent can reference in commands

hint.description: useful description, e.g. 'top face', 'edge along X'

feasibility_notes.description: real constraints useful for feasibility checking (e.g. 5mm thickness) — principle 7
